prompt: Paper-collage stop-motion assembly. Use Image 1 as the exact empty first frame (a flat mustard-yellow paper field) and Image 2 as the exact completed last frame. One continuous locked-off vertical shot, no camera movement. Open on the empty mustard-yellow paper field, then assemble the scene piece by piece with crisp physical stop-motion timing, PACED EVENLY ACROSS THE ENTIRE CLIP: pieces keep sliding in and snapping into place continuously through the whole shot — do NOT finish early, do NOT hold a long static frame; the branch with leaves slides in from the top and settles, then three large fruits with document-textured skins appear one by one and hang low on the branch, and in the final second a relaxed hand slides in from the lower left and closes around the lowest fruit to pluck it. Preserve the 9:16 framing, #B8892A color field, cream-white cardstock and brick-red accents, uncoated paper grain, halftone dots, cream keylines, crisp cut edges and soft shadows. Restrained tactile 2D paper craft only. No scene cuts, no camera movement, no zoom, no morphing, no new objects, no text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermark, no UI, no sound.
